The classification of Anacheilium widgrenii places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae. At the genus level, Anacheilium widgrenii is classified as a member of the genus Anacheilium. intro: Anacheilium widgrenii, also known as the Widow's Plea or Widow's Flower, is a species of orchid native to Central America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Anacheilium widgrenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Anacheilium widgrenii is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract, and is used to treat conditions such as sore throats, coughs, and colds.
